_skill_summary.what: Your daily run-everything command. Replaces what used to be 35+ background tasks (crons).
_skill_summary.why: Used to have 35 separate scheduled jobs across all sites — daily SEO reports, content drafts, autosave commits, ClickUp updates, GBP posts, email digests. All replaced by one session you run yourself.
_skill_summary.how: Type /eros-day in Claude. It runs Phase 0 → Phase 8 in order. Each phase does one type of work (verify, gather data, score health, decide today's plan, write content, etc).
_skill_summary.when: Daily Monday–Friday. Skip weekends. Goal: one session per weekday.
_skill_summary.result: End of session you get an email + Google Doc + ClickUp tasks summarizing exactly what shipped today + what's left.

Phase 0 — Session Start Verify.plain: Quick health check before doing real work. Reads todo lists, checks if any Lovable site is broken for Google, scans for 'no-loans' rule violations, confirms last week's bail-report data isn't stale, and surfaces standing reminders Angelo deferred. Takes about a minute. If something's wrong, you find out NOW instead of mid-blog.

Phase 1 — Data Refresh *(replaces GSC +.plain: Pulls fresh data from Google so you know what people actually searched for. Per-site GSC reports (which keywords brought visitors), GA4 (what visitors did), syncs the keyword bank Google Sheet, computes today's keyword priorities, and runs the Semrush low-hanging-fruit ranking against the local XLSX. Output of this phase decides what gets written in Phase 4.

Phase 2 — Kaizen + Health *(replaces SEO.plain: Scores every site (1–100 + S/A/B/C/D tier) and flags problems. Looks for duplicate content across the 31 microsites (Google hates near-identical pages — 'doorway page' penalty). Builds the active-tiers.json that Phase 3 uses to decide where to invest today's time.

Phase 3 — Triage + Decide.plain: Picks today's bucket of sites and decides what each one needs. A-tier gets a meta rewrite (existing rankings + bad CTR = fast win). B-tier gets a new blog. C-tier gets an infra fix (GBP, schema, redirect). D-tier waits. Output: a 5-line plan. Auto-execute is ON — Angelo said 'run the whole thing' so /eros-day doesn't pause for approval.

Phase 4 — Apply Content.plain: The actual writing + publishing. Pre-write gates first: no-loans audit, fact-check via WebSearch, E-E-A-T checklist. Then write 4–8 microsite blogs OR 2–4 main-site blogs OR meta rewrites OR all of the above. Deploy via wrangler. Verify each URL is live (200 + 400+ words). This is the primary deliverable of every weekday.

Phase 5 — GBP Posts *(Mondays only, repl.plain: Mondays only — generate Google Business Profile posts for ReEnergized. Other Sean sites skip GBP work for now. Skip rule: never rename a GBP. Use parentOrganization schema + weekly posts + geotagged photos instead.

Phase 6 — Email Digest *(replaces daily.plain: Send Angelo the daily summary email. Always to 4434lifeline@gmail.com only (never third parties). Subject sanitized for Gmail, body converted from markdown to proper HTML tables.

Phase 7 — ClickUp Sync.plain: Update task tracker. Close stale duplicates from old crons. Create a new task for today's session. Mark today's work complete or pending-review. Always assign Angelo. Every status change gets a comment so future-EROS can see why it moved.

Phase 8 — Session End *(replaces all 35.plain: Wrap up: commit + push every workspace that has changes, refresh the blog-links sheet, sync the keyword bank, build site-changelog + apply-ratio + strategy-results + dashboard-sync, then generate the EROS Day Final Report (30+ sections). Email to Angelo + Google Doc copy.

Phase 9 — Continuous Self-Research *(Mon.plain: Mondays only — WebSearch latest SEO/AEO/GEO/hyperlocal/Claude API best practices. Rotates 3-4 topics weekly through named sources (Edward Sturm, Matt Diamante, AEO, GEO, hyperlocal). Saves findings.md to data/research/<date>/. Dashboard 'Research' tab surfaces all findings automatically. Surfaces 2-3 actionable improvements per run that feed the next session's Apply Loop.

_skill_summary.what: Weekly report on the entire 215-domain bail network.
_skill_summary.why: Network is too big to handle daily. Once a week — Mondays — refresh probe data, GSC pull, score all 215 domains, build the strategic snapshot.
_skill_summary.how: Type /bail-reports. Runs the data refresh if stale, scores all 215, writes a new sheet tab (Report YYYY-Www, never overwrite prior weeks), sends email with comprehensive S→D breakdown.
_skill_summary.when: Manual. Suggested Monday. First Monday of each month: full refresh including HTTP probe + GSC multi-window. Other Mondays: reuse cached data.
_skill_summary.result: Email + new weekly tab in the master sheet. /eros-day Section #20 references this output as its 5-line summary; Section #24 mirrors the full detail.

Always comprehensive — no tier focus.plain: Every run produces the SAME report — full S→D tier breakdown, all three groups (Bail / Sean Sites / ReEnergized) reported separately, time-period comparisons, top 10 by score, decom shortlist. No 'focus on one tier' mode — that was confusing and got removed.

Hard rules.plain: Three groups, NEVER blended (reenergized used to inflate bail numbers by 98% — that mistake stays permanently fixed). Every tier shows in the email body. Sheet tabs are additive (never overwrite). Time-period comparison required.

_skill_summary.what: Audit yesterday's /eros-day report for errors and check blog cadence.
_skill_summary.why: Reports can ship with `undefined` values, broken tables, or missed sites. This catches the regressions before they pile up.
_skill_summary.how: Run /eros-report-check after /eros-day. Scans the latest report for error markers + checks blog cadence per surface (Lovable, microsites, ReEnergized). Outputs prescriptive fixes that get folded back into /eros-day.
_skill_summary.when: End of any /eros-day session or any time you want a sanity check.
_skill_summary.result: Inline summary under 200 words + full detail at data/daily/<today>/report-check.md.
